commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f67f423e020b7bde3682c4065cdf431a2ec9dbe4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR lowers the automatic read-tool paging budget for known small-context models and adds a regression test covering a 16k-context sandboxed read.

PR surface: Source +17, Tests +25. Total +42 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main passes modelContextWindowTokens into createOpenClawReadTool, but resolveAdaptiveReadMaxBytes floors a 16k context back to DEFAULT_READ_PAGE_MAX_BYTES. I did not run a live failing test in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This draft maintainer PR has protected handling plus a narrow correctness finding, so a maintainer should revise or explicitly authorize a focused repair before merge.

target.title: fix(agents): scale read output for small contexts
target.body:
Summary:
- Scale the default read-tool auto paging cap down when the active model advertises a small context window.
- Keep the existing 32KB default when no model context is known, and keep explicit `read(limit=...)` calls authoritative.
- Add a focused regression test for a 16k-context model so large file reads return a small continuation page instead of a large default page.

Verification:
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx -e '<read small-context smoke>'`
- GitHub draft helper checks: 7 passed, 0 failed, 63 skipped/cancelled by draft gating.

Behavior addressed: small/local model turns can lose too much prompt budget to a default file read page even though the runner already knows the model context window.
Real environment tested: local linked gwt worktree on current `origin/main` at `def11c097800362357092d2935648c00ecad805f`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: created an 8,000-line temp file and called `createSandboxedReadTool` once with `modelContextWindowTokens: 16000` and once without model context.
Evidence after fix: small-context read returned `smallLen: 4791` with continuation guidance; no-context read kept the existing `baseLen: 51244` / `capped at 32KB` behavior.
Observed result after fix: the small-context read no longer delivers the old large default page and still tells the model how to continue with `offset`.
What was not tested: full Vitest file execution locally; the Codex linked worktree Vitest process pegged a core with no output and was stopped. Crabbox/Testbox proof was attempted through `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --shell -- "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.adds-claude-style-aliases-schemas-without-dropping-g.test.ts"`, but the wrapper failed before remote dispatch while preparing its local full sync checkout: `No space left on device`.

Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
